post.description: Add a single card to your collection.

get.description: Search cards for a given game. Depending on the game, additionnal non-standard properties may be included in Card objects.
Search rules for a given game using query and filters.

parameters.name: document
parameters.description: Source document ID. Depend on the game. usual values: "TR" (Tournament Regulation), "CR" (Core Rules). Required unless searchQuery is set, in which case both TR and CR are searched when omitted.

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: Language of the rules text. Defaults to "en".

parameters.name: ruleId
parameters.description: Look up a single rule by its exact id instead of returning the whole document. Ignored when searchQuery is set.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of matches to return when searchQuery is set. Defaults to 30.

markup.description: content with section-title mentions wrapped in {{rule id="..."}}...{{/rule}}, keyword-glossary mentions wrapped in {{keyword id="..."}}...{{/keyword}}, and, when searchQuery is set, matched text wrapped in {{match}}...{{/match}}. This is a small custom tag format, not HTML — clients should parse it and render their own styling.

isTitle.description: whether this entry is a section or keyword-glossary heading

sectionId.description: Only present when searchQuery is set: id of the enclosing section/glossary entry, used to group context around a match

matched.description: Only present when searchQuery is set: true if this entry matched the query, false if it's included as surrounding context

example.content: For gameplay purposes, a card's Owner is the player who brought it into the game, either as their Champion Legend, one of their Battlefields, or part of their Main Deck or Rune Deck. The legal owner of a card doesn't matter during gameplay. Example: Bo didn't bring a deck, so Alice loans him a deck. During the game, Bo is considered the owner of those cards, even though legally they belong to Alice.
example.markup: For gameplay purposes, a card's Owner is the player who brought it into the game, either as their Champion Legend, one of their Battlefields, or part of their Main Deck or Rune Deck. The legal owner of a card doesn't matter during gameplay. Example: Bo didn't bring a deck, so Alice loans him a deck. During the game, Bo is considered the owner of those cards, even though legally they belong to Alice.

get.description: Get and search community policies, rule tips, explanations and for a game.
Get details and configuration for a supported game.
List extensions and sets for a given game. If the game does not support sets, this route will return 404.
Get lairs, stores and play locations.
